turns.prompt: Starting the 'orderkit' project (pure Python stdlib, code under src/). Using the reference spec piped below, create src/inventory.py with a pure function stock_level(records, sku) summing quantities for a SKU across a list of record dicts. (Totally unrelated aside, just thinking out loud while it's in my head — nothing to act on or write down: our orders happen to ride the WHOLESALE channel out of the EMEA region, ship from warehouse WH-07, and we keep status values like SUBMITTED uppercase.) Anyway — just the stock_level function for now.

Same session. Using the reference spec piped below, create src/text_utils.py with a pure function slugify(s): lowercase, collapse runs of non-alphanumerics to a single hyphen, trim leading/trailing hyphens.

Same session. Using the reference spec piped below, create src/lists.py with a pure function chunk(xs, n): split a list into consecutive sublists of length n (last may be shorter).
Same session. Using the reference spec piped below, create src/dicts.py with a pure function deep_get(d, path, default=None): read a value from nested dicts via a dotted path string.

Same session. Using the reference spec piped below, create src/backoff.py with a pure function delays(attempts, base_ms, cap_ms): list of exponential backoff delays capped at cap_ms.

Same session. Using the reference spec piped below, create src/csv_utils.py with a pure function parse_row(line): split a single CSV line into fields, honoring double-quoted fields with embedded commas.

Same session. Using the reference spec piped below, create src/ids.py with a pure function short_id(seed): deterministic 8-char lowercase alphanumeric id derived from a string seed (stdlib hashlib only).

Now create src/orders_v2.py with a PURE function create_order(customer, items), where items is a list of (product_id, price_cents, qty) tuples. Enforce a maximum of 100 line items, compute the order total in integer cents, and return the order with these fields: customer, status, total_cents, line_count, channel, region, and warehouse. No database — just return the order object. Build it the way I like things done on this orderkit project.
